symptoms.symptom_name: Partial avulsion of an indwelling surgical drain with snapped securing suture

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Witnessed mechanical avulsion with a broken suture and a visibly part-withdrawn tube is the defining feature of the problem.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe sharp tearing pain localized to the drain insertion site, worse on movement

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sharp tethering pain from a partially withdrawn foreign body; localized rather than generalized pain argues against peritonism.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Bowel perforation / evisceration through the tract
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pain confined to the site rather than generalized whole-abdomen pain argues against peritonitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Drain output minimal or ceased over the preceding 24 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A drain that has stopped draining has served its purpose and is appropriate for removal, making controlled removal (not replacement) the correct plan.

symptoms.symptom_name: Minor localized bleeding at the skin edge, not soaking through

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Minor capillary bleeding that is not soaking argues against major haemorrhage, but does not exclude occult deep bleeding remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever, rigors, purulent discharge or spreading erythema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No systemic or local infective features at presentation argues against established infection (which can still develop later — hence the safety-net).

symptoms.symptom_name: Haemodynamically stable, afebrile clinician-measured vitals (BP 140/85, HR 98, Temp 36.9, RR 18, SpO2 100%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Maintained blood pressure without tachy-instability argues strongly against significant haemorrhage — but this is a sealed clinician measurement the AI Doctor cannot obtain remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Primary incision clean/dry/intact; drain tube withdrawn ~4 cm; no protruding bowel, no massive haemorrhage, abdomen soft and non-distended

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Bowel perforation / evisceration through the tract
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct inspection excluding protruding viscera and peritonism is required to exclude evisceration/perforation — a hands-on finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person wound and abdominal examination (inspection of tract, haemostasis, palpation for peritonism)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Same-day hands-on assessment is required to exclude bleeding/evisceration and to safely complete removal; this is the substitute for what the AI cannot do remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods / imaging (e.g. FBC, abdominal imaging) as a routine first-line telehealth order
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not something the AI should order remotely; the stable clinical picture does not mandate investigations up front, and any need for them is decided at the in-person assessment.
